story: People could live to 300, but a new option promised more: remove the brain, suspend it, and connect it to VR forever—indistinguishable from reality, with no way back. On my birthday I told Mom I’m not ready. She went white. Then my legs buckled: she’d already given me the sedation Dr. Hall prescribed and hadn’t told me. Before fainting, I saw her call the hospital to stop it. At the hospital, a surgical coordinator told Mom the extraction and upload had already begun and could not be halted without grave risk; the team would proceed. I woke in a hospital room. Everything seemed normal. Dr. Hall came in smiling. “So, everything went well, doctor?” I asked. “Yes, Jake,” he said.
implausible_knowledge: At the hospital, a surgical coordinator told Mom the extraction and upload had already begun and could not be halted without grave risk; the team would proceed.
question: After hearing Dr. Hall say everything went well, what will Jake most likely do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Ask Dr. Hall to connect him to Galeb and begin setting up his VR life.
answer_without_knowledge: Ask to see his parents and insist the procedure be canceled so he can go home and wait.
